gazette: The Official Journal of the European Union OJ L 159I , 23.6.2023
title: The European Union (EU) has updated its regulations regarding sanctions on Russia
bodyHtml:
The European Union (EU) has updated its regulations regarding sanctions on Russia. In order to prevent circumvention of the sanctions, cooperation with third countries will be enhanced. If the cooperation efforts do not yield results, the EU will take rapid and targeted measures against institutions in third countries that contribute to Russia.

In cases where attempts to bypass EU measures continue despite negotiations and individual sanctions, exceptional measures will be implemented. In this context, the European Council may restrict the sale, supply, transfer, or export of goods and technologies that are prohibited from being sent to Russia to third countries. Third countries that supply Russia with goods and technologies, particularly those used in a war zone, while evading EU sanctions, will fall under this scope.

The list of prohibited items that could contribute to Russia's technological development in the defense and security sector will also be expanded. Electronic components, semiconductors, electronic circuit and board manufacturing equipment, various chemicals, optical components, metals, and navigation instruments and maritime equipment will be added to the list of prohibited items.

For the Council Regulation 2023/1214 that introduces these changes, click here.
